text.title: Google Ads Reporting and Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-google-reporting methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Optimize on conversion-tied metrics (CPA, ROAS, conversion count). Clicks, impressions, CTR are diagnostic — never targets.
rule.rationale: More clicks at higher CPA destroys margin; optimizing on vanity metrics is the dominant cause of budget waste.

rule.statement: The Search Terms report is reviewed weekly cost-descending; every term over $5 is classified keep / negate / promote.
rule.rationale: Irrelevant searches accumulate weekly; Search Terms is the earliest detector of waste.

rule.statement: Major structural changes (bids ±25%, ad-group restructure, new bidding strategy) MUST be limited to monthly cadence with ≥2 weeks of stable data after each change.
rule.rationale: Google's bidding models need stable signal; daily churn resets the learning phase.

rule.statement: Before judging a campaign healthy/unhealthy, segment by device, network, location, audience, time. Aggregate averages MUST NOT be used in isolation.
rule.rationale: Campaign-level averages hide high- and low-performing slices.

rule.statement: Keywords with Quality Score under 5 are flagged for ad-group split, ad copy rewrite, or landing-page realignment before any bid change.
rule.rationale: Low QS multiplies effective CPC; fixing QS is cheaper than out-bidding the competition.
